subject: Physics
q: A long solenoid has 200 turns per $\mathrm{cm}$ and carries a current $\mathrm{i}$. The magnetic field at its centre is $6.28 \times 10^{-2}$ Weber $/ \mathrm{m}^2$. Another long solenoid has 100 turns per $\mathrm{cm}$ and it carries a current $\mathrm{i} / 3$. The value of the magnetic field at its centre is
question: A long solenoid has 200 turns per $\mathrm{cm}$ and carries a current $\mathrm{i}$. The magnetic field at its centre is $6.28 \times 10^{-2}$ Weber $/ \mathrm{m}^2$. Another long solenoid has 100 turns per $\mathrm{cm}$ and it carries a current $\mathrm{i} / 3$. The value of the magnetic field at its centre is
solution: $B_2=\frac{B_1 n_2 i_2}{n_1 i_1}=\frac{\left(6.28 \times 10^{-2}\right)(100 \times i / 3)}{200(i)}=1.05 \times 10^{-2} \mathrm{~W} / \mathrm{m}^2$

subject: Physics
q: A very long wire ABDMNDC is shown in figure carrying current I. AB and BC parts are straight, long and at right angle. At D wire forms a circular turn DMND of radius R. AB, BC parts are tangential to circular turn at N and D. Magnetic filed at the center of circle is:
question: A very long wire ABDMNDC is shown in figure carrying current I. AB and BC parts are straight, long and at right angle. At D wire forms a circular turn DMND of radius R. AB, BC parts are tangential to circular turn at N and D. Magnetic filed at the center of circle is:
solution:
B0→=B0→1+B0→2+B0→3+B0→4
μ0i4πRsin⁡90°-sin45°⨂+μ0i2R⨀+ μ0i4πR(sin45°+sin90°)⨀
-μ0i4πR1-12+μ0i2R+μ0i4πR12+1⨀
=μ0i4πR-1+12+2π+12+1⨀=μ0i4πR2+2π⨀=μ0i2πR12+π⨀

subject: Physics
q: A coil having 100 turns, area of $5 \times 10^{-3} \mathrm{~m}^2$, carrying current of $1 \mathrm{~mA}$ is placed in uniform magnetic field of $0.20 \mathrm{~T}$ such a way that plane of coil is perpendicular to the magnetic field. The work done in turning the coil through $90^{\circ}$ is _______ $\mu \mathrm{J}$.
question: A coil having 100 turns, area of $5 \times 10^{-3} \mathrm{~m}^2$, carrying current of $1 \mathrm{~mA}$ is placed in uniform magnetic field of $0.20 \mathrm{~T}$ such a way that plane of coil is perpendicular to the magnetic field. The work done in turning the coil through $90^{\circ}$ is _______ $\mu \mathrm{J}$.
solution: $\begin{aligned} & W=\Delta U=U_f-U_i \\ & W=(-\vec{\mu} . \vec{B})_f-(-\vec{\mu} . \vec{B})_i \\ & =0+(\vec{\mu} \cdot \vec{B})_i \\ & =\left(100 \times 5 \times 10^{-3} \times 1 \times 10^{-3}\right) \times 0.2 \mathrm{~J} \\ & =1 \times 10^{-4} \mathrm{~J}=100 \mu \mathrm{J}\end{aligned}$

subject: Physics
q: An iron rod of volume <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mn>10</mn><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>3</mn></mrow></msup><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mn>3</mn></msup></math> and relative permeability <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1000</mn></math> is placed as core in a solenoid with<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>10</mn><mo> </mo><mi>turns</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi>cm</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math> . If a current of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>5</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math> is passed through the solenoid, then the magnetic moment of the rod will be :
question: An iron rod of volume <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mn>10</mn><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>3</mn></mrow></msup><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mn>3</mn></msup></math> and relative permeability <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1000</mn></math> is placed as core in a solenoid with<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>10</mn><mo> </mo><mi>turns</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi>cm</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math> . If a current of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>5</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math> is passed through the solenoid, then the magnetic moment of the rod will be :
solution:
Given,

Volume of iron rod is, V=10-3 m3

Relative permeability, μr=1000

No of turns per unit length of solenoid is, n=Nl=10 turns cm-1=1000 turns m-1

Current through the solenoid is, i=0.5 A

Magnetic moment of an iron core solenoid is given by,

M=(μr-1) NiA

⇒M=μr-1NiVl=μr-1NliV

⇒M=1000-1×1000×0.5×10-3

⇒M=499.5≈500 A m2

subject: Physics
q: Two long parallel wires carrying currents <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>8</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>15</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math> in opposite directions are placed at a distance of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>7</mn><mo> </mo><mi>cm</mi></math> from each other. A point <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>P</mi></math> is at equidistant from both the wires such that the lines joining the point <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>P</mi></math> to the wires are perpendicular to each other. The magnitude of magnetic field at <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>P</mi></math> is ______ <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>6</mn></mrow></msup><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">T</mi></math>. (Given: <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msqrt><mn>2</mn></msqrt><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>4</mn></math>)
question: Two long parallel wires carrying currents <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>8</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>15</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math> in opposite directions are placed at a distance of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>7</mn><mo> </mo><mi>cm</mi></math> from each other. A point <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>P</mi></math> is at equidistant from both the wires such that the lines joining the point <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>P</mi></math> to the wires are perpendicular to each other. The magnitude of magnetic field at <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>P</mi></math> is ______ <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>6</mn></mrow></msup><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">T</mi></math>. (Given: <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msqrt><mn>2</mn></msqrt><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>4</mn></math>)
solution:
Here, the magnetic fields produced by the two wires will be perpendicular to one another.

Magnetic field due to wire carrying current 8 A is B1=μ0i12πd

and magnetic field due to wire carrying current 15 A is B2=μ0i22πd

The net magnetic field is 

Bnet=B12+B22=μ02πdi12+i22

=4π×10-72π×72×10-2×82+152 d=72 cm

=68×10-6  T.

subject: Physics
q: Two bar magnets oscillate in a horizontal plane in earth's magnetic field with time periods of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>4</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mo> </mo></math>respectively. If their moments of inertia are in the ratio of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn><mo>:</mo><mn>2</mn></math> then the ratio of their magnetic moments will be
question: Two bar magnets oscillate in a horizontal plane in earth's magnetic field with time periods of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>4</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mo> </mo></math>respectively. If their moments of inertia are in the ratio of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn><mo>:</mo><mn>2</mn></math> then the ratio of their magnetic moments will be
solution:
Torque on a bar magnet kept in a magnetic field is given by τ=MBsinθ.

Hence, for small oscillations the time period of bar magnets is given by,

T=2πIMB⇒M∝IT2

⇒M1M2=I1I2×T2T12

⇒M1M2=32×432=83

subject: Physics
q: A charged particle going around in a circle can be considered to be a current loop. A particle of a mass <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></math> carrying charge <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">q</mi></math> is moving in a plane with speed <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>v</mi></math> under the influence of magnetic field <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mover><mi>B</mi><mo>→</mo></mover></math>. The magnetic moment of this moving particle is :
question: A charged particle going around in a circle can be considered to be a current loop. A particle of a mass <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></math> carrying charge <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">q</mi></math> is moving in a plane with speed <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>v</mi></math> under the influence of magnetic field <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mover><mi>B</mi><mo>→</mo></mover></math>. The magnetic moment of this moving particle is :
solution:
i=qT=qv2πr

M=i×πr2

=2v2πr×πr2

=12qv×mvqB

Direction of M→ is opposite of B→ therefore

M→=-mv2B→2B2

subject: Physics
q: A long solenoid carrying a current produces a magnetic field <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>B</mi></math> along its axis. If the current is doubled and the number of turns per <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>cm</mi></math> is halved, the new value of magnetic field will be equal to
question: A long solenoid carrying a current produces a magnetic field <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>B</mi></math> along its axis. If the current is doubled and the number of turns per <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>cm</mi></math> is halved, the new value of magnetic field will be equal to
solution:
Magnetic field due to long solenoid along its axis is given as,

B=μ0nI, where n is the number of turns in the solenoid per unit length, I is current in the solenoid and μ0 is a constant called the permeability of free space.

⇒B1 B2=n1I1n2I2

According to the question, n2=n12 and I2=2I1

⇒BB2=n1n12×I12I1⇒B2=B; means, the magnetic field remains the same.

subject: Physics
q: A current of $30$ A each flows in opposite directions in two conducting wires, placed parallel to each other at a distance of $8$ cm. The magnetic field at the mid point between the two wires is __________ $\mu$T. $\left(\dfrac{\mu_0}{4\pi} = 10^{-7}\text{ N/A}^2\right)$
question: A current of $30$ A each flows in opposite directions in two conducting wires, placed parallel to each other at a distance of $8$ cm. The magnetic field at the mid point between the two wires is __________ $\mu$T. $\left(\dfrac{\mu_0}{4\pi} = 10^{-7}\text{ N/A}^2\right)$
solution:
Let the distance between the wires be $d = 8\text{ cm}$. The distance of the midpoint from each wire is $r = \dfrac{d}{2} = 4\text{ cm} = 0.04\text{ m}$.

The current in each wire is $I = 30\text{ A}$.

Since the currents flow in opposite directions, the magnetic fields produced by both wires at the midpoint point in the same direction and add up.

The total magnetic field at the midpoint is:

$B_{\text{net}} = B_1 + B_2 = \dfrac{\mu_0 I}{2\pi r} + \dfrac{\mu_0 I}{2\pi r} = \dfrac{\mu_0 I}{\pi r}$

Substituting the given values:

$B_{\text{net}} = \dfrac{4\pi \times 10^{-7} \times 30}{\pi \times 0.04}$

$B_{\text{net}} = \dfrac{120 \times 10^{-7}}{0.04}$

$B_{\text{net}} = 3000 \times 10^{-7}\text{ T}$

$B_{\text{net}} = 300 \times 10^{-6}\text{ T} = 300\ \mu\text{T}$

Answer: $300$

q:
The current sensitivity of a galvanometer can be increased by :
(A) decreasing the number of turns
(B) increasing the magnetic field
(C) decreasing the area of the coil
(D) decreasing the torsional constant of the spring

Choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below :
question:
The current sensitivity of a galvanometer can be increased by :
(A) decreasing the number of turns
(B) increasing the magnetic field
(C) decreasing the area of the coil
(D) decreasing the torsional constant of the spring

Choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below :
solution:
The current in the galvanometer is calculated by i=KNABθ.

Therefore, the sensitivity of the galvanometer,

dθdi=NABK

Hence, only options B and D are correct.

subject: Physics
q: A small cube of side $1$ mm is placed at the centre of a circular loop of radius $10$ cm carrying a current of $2$ A. The magnetic energy stored inside the cube is $\alpha \times 10^{-14}$ J. The value of $\alpha$ is _______. ($\mu_o = 4\pi \times 10^{-7}$ Tm/A, $\pi = 3.14$)
question: A small cube of side $1$ mm is placed at the centre of a circular loop of radius $10$ cm carrying a current of $2$ A. The magnetic energy stored inside the cube is $\alpha \times 10^{-14}$ J. The value of $\alpha$ is _______. ($\mu_o = 4\pi \times 10^{-7}$ Tm/A, $\pi = 3.14$)
solution:
The magnetic field at the centre of the circular loop is given by $B = \dfrac{\mu_0 I}{2R}$

Substituting the given values, $B = \dfrac{4\pi \times 10^{-7} \times 2}{2 \times 0.1} = 4\pi \times 10^{-6}$ T

The magnetic energy density is $u = \dfrac{B^2}{2\mu_0}$

$u = \dfrac{(4\pi \times 10^{-6})^2}{2 \times 4\pi \times 10^{-7}} = \dfrac{16\pi^2 \times 10^{-12}}{8\pi \times 10^{-7}} = 2\pi \times 10^{-5}$ J/m$^3$

The volume of the small cube is $V = (1 \text{ mm})^3 = (10^{-3} \text{ m})^3 = 10^{-9}$ m$^3$

The magnetic energy stored inside the cube is $U = u \times V$

$U = 2\pi \times 10^{-5} \times 10^{-9} = 2\pi \times 10^{-14}$ J

Using $\pi = 3.14$, we get $U = 2 \times 3.14 \times 10^{-14} = 6.28 \times 10^{-14}$ J

Comparing with $\alpha \times 10^{-14}$ J, we get $\alpha = 6.28$

Answer: $6.28$

subject: Physics
q: A positive charge ' $\mathrm{q}$ ' of mass ' $\mathrm{m}$ ' is moving along the $+x$ axis. We wish to apply a uniform magnetic field B for time $\Delta t$ so that the charge reverses its direction crossing the $\mathrm{y}$ axis at a distance $\mathrm{d}$. Then:
question: A positive charge ' $\mathrm{q}$ ' of mass ' $\mathrm{m}$ ' is moving along the $+x$ axis. We wish to apply a uniform magnetic field B for time $\Delta t$ so that the charge reverses its direction crossing the $\mathrm{y}$ axis at a distance $\mathrm{d}$. Then:
solution:
The applied magnetic field provides the required centripetal force to the charge particle, so it can move in circular path of radius $\frac{\mathrm{d}}{2}$
$$
\therefore \quad B q v=\frac{m^2}{d / 2}
$$
or, $\quad B=\frac{2 \mathrm{mv}}{\mathrm{qd}}$
Time interval for which a uniform magnetic field is applied
$$
\Delta \mathrm{t}=\frac{\pi \cdot \frac{\mathrm{d}}{2}}{\mathrm{v}}
$$
(particle reverses its direction after time $\Delta \mathrm{t}$ by covering semi circle).
$$
\Delta \mathrm{t}=\frac{\pi \mathrm{d}}{2 \mathrm{v}}
$$

subject: Physics
q: $1\,\mu$C charge moving with velocity $\vec{v} = \left(\hat{i} - 2\hat{j} + 3\hat{k}\right)$ m/s in the region of magnetic field $\vec{B} = \left(2\hat{i} + 3\hat{j} - 5\hat{k}\right)$ T. The magnitude of force acting on it is $\sqrt{\alpha} \times 10^{-6}$ N. The value of $\alpha$ is _______.
question: $1\,\mu$C charge moving with velocity $\vec{v} = \left(\hat{i} - 2\hat{j} + 3\hat{k}\right)$ m/s in the region of magnetic field $\vec{B} = \left(2\hat{i} + 3\hat{j} - 5\hat{k}\right)$ T. The magnitude of force acting on it is $\sqrt{\alpha} \times 10^{-6}$ N. The value of $\alpha$ is _______.
solution:
The magnetic force acting on a moving charge is given by $\vec{F} = q(\vec{v} \times \vec{B})$.

Given:
$q = 1 \, \mu\text{C} = 10^{-6} \text{ C}$
$\vec{v} = \hat{i} - 2\hat{j} + 3\hat{k}$
$\vec{B} = 2\hat{i} + 3\hat{j} - 5\hat{k}$

Calculating the cross product $\vec{v} \times \vec{B}$:
$\vec{v} \times \vec{B} = \begin{vmatrix} \hat{i} & \hat{j} & \hat{k} \\ 1 & -2 & 3 \\ 2 & 3 & -5 \end{vmatrix}$

$\vec{v} \times \vec{B} = \hat{i}(10 - 9) - \hat{j}(-5 - 6) + \hat{k}(3 + 4)$
$\vec{v} \times \vec{B} = \hat{i} + 11\hat{j} + 7\hat{k}$

The magnitude of $\vec{v} \times \vec{B}$ is:
$|\vec{v} \times \vec{B}| = \sqrt{1^2 + 11^2 + 7^2} = \sqrt{1 + 121 + 49} = \sqrt{171}$

The magnitude of the force is:
$|\vec{F}| = q |\vec{v} \times \vec{B}| = 10^{-6} \times \sqrt{171} = \sqrt{171} \times 10^{-6} \text{ N}$

Comparing this with the given expression $\sqrt{\alpha} \times 10^{-6} \text{ N}$, we get:
$\alpha = 171$

Answer: $171$

subject: Physics
q: A charge particle moves along circular path in a uniform magnetic field in a cyclotron. The kinetic energy of the charge particle increases to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>4</mn></math> times its initial value. What will be the ratio of new radius to the original radius of circular path of the charge particle?
question: A charge particle moves along circular path in a uniform magnetic field in a cyclotron. The kinetic energy of the charge particle increases to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>4</mn></math> times its initial value. What will be the ratio of new radius to the original radius of circular path of the charge particle?
solution:
Radius of a charged particle moving in a magnetic field perpendicular to its velocity is given by, R=mvqB.

mv can be written in terms of kinetic energy as mv=2mKE. Therefore,

R=mvqB=2mKEqB

⇒R∝KE⇒R2R1=K2K1=41=21

subject: Physics
q: A charged particle carrying charge <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo> </mo><mi>μ</mi><mi>C</mi></math> is moving with velocity <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mn>2</mn><mover><mi>i</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>+</mo><mn>3</mn><mover><mi>j</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>+</mo><mn>4</mn><mover><mi>k</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>)</mo><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math>. If an external magnetic field of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mn>5</mn><mover><mi>i</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>+</mo><mn>3</mn><mover><mi>j</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>-</mo><mn>6</mn><mover><mi>k</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>)</mo><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>3</mn></mrow></msup><mi>T</mi></math> exists in the region where the particle is moving then the force on the particle is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mover><mi mathvariant="normal">F</mi><mo>→</mo></mover><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>9</mn></mrow></msup><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi></math> . the vector <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mover><mi mathvariant="normal">F</mi><mo>→</mo></mover></math> is :
question: A charged particle carrying charge <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo> </mo><mi>μ</mi><mi>C</mi></math> is moving with velocity <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mn>2</mn><mover><mi>i</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>+</mo><mn>3</mn><mover><mi>j</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>+</mo><mn>4</mn><mover><mi>k</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>)</mo><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math>. If an external magnetic field of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mn>5</mn><mover><mi>i</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>+</mo><mn>3</mn><mover><mi>j</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>-</mo><mn>6</mn><mover><mi>k</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>)</mo><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>3</mn></mrow></msup><mi>T</mi></math> exists in the region where the particle is moving then the force on the particle is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mover><mi mathvariant="normal">F</mi><mo>→</mo></mover><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>9</mn></mrow></msup><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi></math> . the vector <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mover><mi mathvariant="normal">F</mi><mo>→</mo></mover></math> is :
solution:
Using the equation of force as,

F→=qV→×B→

F→=10-6×10-3i^j^k^23453-6

=-30i^+32j^-9k^×10-9 N

subject: Physics
q: Proton, Deuteron and alpha particle of the same kinetic energy are moving in circular trajectories in a constant magnetic field. The radii of proton, deuteron and alpha particle are respectively $r_p, r_d$ and $r_\alpha$. Which one of the following relations is correct?
question: Proton, Deuteron and alpha particle of the same kinetic energy are moving in circular trajectories in a constant magnetic field. The radii of proton, deuteron and alpha particle are respectively $r_p, r_d$ and $r_\alpha$. Which one of the following relations is correct?
solution:
$r=\frac{\sqrt{2 m K}}{B q}$
$r \propto \frac{\sqrt{m}}{q}$
$r_\alpha=r_p < r_d$

subject: Physics
q: One of the two identical conducting wires of length <math><mi>L</mi></math> is bent in the form of a circular loop and the other one into a circular coil of <math><mi>N</mi></math> identical turns. If the same current is passed in both, the ratio of the magnetic field at the centre of the loop <math><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><msub><mrow><mi>B</mi></mrow><mrow><mi>L</mi></mrow></msub></mrow></mfenced></math> to that at the centre of the coil <math><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><msub><mrow><mi>B</mi></mrow><mrow><mi>C</mi></mrow></msub></mrow></mfenced><mo>,</mo></math> i.e. <math><mfrac><mrow><msub><mrow><mi>B</mi></mrow><mrow><mi>L</mi></mrow></msub></mrow><mrow><msub><mrow><mi>B</mi></mrow><mrow><mi>C</mi></mrow></msub></mrow></mfrac></math> will be
question: One of the two identical conducting wires of length <math><mi>L</mi></math> is bent in the form of a circular loop and the other one into a circular coil of <math><mi>N</mi></math> identical turns. If the same current is passed in both, the ratio of the magnetic field at the centre of the loop <math><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><msub><mrow><mi>B</mi></mrow><mrow><mi>L</mi></mrow></msub></mrow></mfenced></math> to that at the centre of the coil <math><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><msub><mrow><mi>B</mi></mrow><mrow><mi>C</mi></mrow></msub></mrow></mfenced><mo>,</mo></math> i.e. <math><mfrac><mrow><msub><mrow><mi>B</mi></mrow><mrow><mi>L</mi></mrow></msub></mrow><mrow><msub><mrow><mi>B</mi></mrow><mrow><mi>C</mi></mrow></msub></mrow></mfrac></math> will be
solution:
For a single loop, radius

r1=L2π

BL=μ0I2r1=μ0I2L×2π  ...(1)

For a circular coil, radius

r2=L2πN

Bc=μ0IN2r2=μ0IN2L2πN  ...(2)

BLBC=1N2             (from eqns (1) and (2))

subject: Physics
q:
An electron is moving along the positive x-axis. If uniform magnetic field is applied parallel to the negative z-axis, then

A.The electron will experience magnetic force along positive y-axis

B.The electron will experience magnetic force along negative y-axis

C.The electron will not experience any force in magnetic field

D.The electron will continue to move along the positive x-axis

E.The electron will move along circular path in magnetic field

Choose the correct answer from the option given below:
question:
An electron is moving along the positive x-axis. If uniform magnetic field is applied parallel to the negative z-axis, then

A.The electron will experience magnetic force along positive y-axis

B.The electron will experience magnetic force along negative y-axis

C.The electron will not experience any force in magnetic field

D.The electron will continue to move along the positive x-axis

E.The electron will move along circular path in magnetic field

Choose the correct answer from the option given below:
solution:
The Lorentz force is given by

F→=qv→×B→

=-ev→×B→

So, F^=i^×k^=-j^.

F→ is  along -y axis 

As the magnetic field is perpendicular to the velocity, motion would be circular.

subject: Physics
q: A proton, a deuteron and an <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi></math> particle are moving with same momentum in a uniform magnetic field. The ratio of magnetic forces acting on them ______ is and their speed is ______ in the ratio.
question: A proton, a deuteron and an <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi></math> particle are moving with same momentum in a uniform magnetic field. The ratio of magnetic forces acting on them ______ is and their speed is ______ in the ratio.
solution:
F=qv→×B→=qmP→×B→

⇒F∝qm

thus,
 F1:F2:F3=q1 m1:q2 m2:q3 m3=emp:e2mp:2e4mp=11:12:24=2:1:1

Now for speed calculation, P=constant⇒v∝1 m

thus v1:v2:v3=1 mp:12mp:14mp=11:12:14=4:2:1.

subject: Physics
q: The fractional change in the magnetic field intensity at a distance <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>r</mi></math> from centre on the axis of current carrying coil of radius <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>a</mi></math> to the magnetic field intensity at the centre of the same coil is: (Take <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">r</mi><mo><</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi></math>)
question: The fractional change in the magnetic field intensity at a distance <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>r</mi></math> from centre on the axis of current carrying coil of radius <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>a</mi></math> to the magnetic field intensity at the centre of the same coil is: (Take <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">r</mi><mo><</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi></math>)
solution:
As we know,

Baxis=μ0iR22R2+x23/2  where, R=a  

Bcentre =μ0i2a

∴Baxis =μ0ia22a2+r23/2

∴ fractional change in magnetic field =μ0i2a-μ0ia22a2+r23/2μ0i2a=1-11+r2a23/2

≈1−1−32r2a2=32r2a2

subject: Physics
q: An electron projected perpendicular to a uniform magnetic field B moves in a circle. If Bohr's quantization is applicable, then the radius of the electronic orbit in the first excited state is :
question: An electron projected perpendicular to a uniform magnetic field B moves in a circle. If Bohr's quantization is applicable, then the radius of the electronic orbit in the first excited state is :
solution:
$m v r=\frac{n h}{2 \pi}$ ...(i)
$r=\frac{v m}{B q}$ ....(ii)
$\begin{aligned} & n=2 \\ & m r\left(\frac{r B q}{m}\right)=\frac{2 h}{2 \pi} \\ & r=\sqrt{\frac{h}{\pi B q}} \\ & q=e \\ & r=\sqrt{\frac{h}{\pi B e}}\end{aligned}$

subject: Physics
q: An electron is moving along <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>+</mo><mi>x</mi></math> direction with a velocity of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>6</mn><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mn>6</mn></msup><msup><mi>ms</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math>. It enters a region of uniform electric field of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>300</mn><mo> </mo><mi>V</mi><mo>/</mo><mi>c</mi><mi>m</mi></math> pointing along <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>+</mo><mi>y</mi></math> direction. The magnitude and direction of the magnetic field set up in this region such that the electron keeps moving along the x direction will be:
question: An electron is moving along <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>+</mo><mi>x</mi></math> direction with a velocity of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>6</mn><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mn>6</mn></msup><msup><mi>ms</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math>. It enters a region of uniform electric field of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>300</mn><mo> </mo><mi>V</mi><mo>/</mo><mi>c</mi><mi>m</mi></math> pointing along <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>+</mo><mi>y</mi></math> direction. The magnitude and direction of the magnetic field set up in this region such that the electron keeps moving along the x direction will be:
solution:
q∨B=qE

B=EV=300006×106=5×103T

Magnetic force F=q(V→×B→)

So Ans.5×103T ,along +Z direction

subject: Physics
q: A rectangular loop of wire, supporting a mass $m$, hangs with one end in a uniform magnetic field $\vec{B}$ pointing out of the plane of the paper. A clockwise current is set up such that $i>m g / B a$, where $a$ is the width of the loop. Then :
question: A rectangular loop of wire, supporting a mass $m$, hangs with one end in a uniform magnetic field $\vec{B}$ pointing out of the plane of the paper. A clockwise current is set up such that $i>m g / B a$, where $a$ is the width of the loop. Then :
options: The weight rises due to a vertical force caused by the magnetic field and work is done on the system.
The weight do not rise due to vertical for caused by the magnetic field and work is done on the system.
The weight rises due to a vertical force caused by the magnetic field but no work is done on the system.
The weight rises due to a vertical force caused by the magnetic field and work is extracted from the magnetic field.
solution: No Solution Available

q: A coil in the shape of an equilateral triangle of side <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>10</mn><mo> </mo><mi>cm</mi></math> lies in a vertical plane between the pole pieces of permanent magnet producing a horizontal magnetic field <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>20</mn><mi>mT</mi></math>. The value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi></math> is
question: A coil in the shape of an equilateral triangle of side <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>10</mn><mo> </mo><mi>cm</mi></math> lies in a vertical plane between the pole pieces of permanent magnet producing a horizontal magnetic field <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>20</mn><mi>mT</mi></math>. The torque acting on the coil when a current of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>2</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math> is passed through it and its plane becomes parallel to the magnetic field will be <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msqrt><mi>x</mi></msqrt><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>5</mn></mrow></msup><mi>Nm</mi></math>. The value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi></math> is
solution:
τ=M→×B→

=IAB

=0.2×3a24×2×10-2=0.2×3×10-2×2×10-24

=3×10-5 N m

subject: Physics
q:
If the maximum value of accelerating potential provided by a radio frequency oscillator is 12kV. The number of revolution made by a proton in a cyclotron to achieve one sixth of the speed of light is: 

mp=1.67×10-27 kg, e=1.6×10-19 C, Speed of light=3×108 m s-1
question:
If the maximum value of accelerating potential provided by a radio frequency oscillator is 12kV. The number of revolution made by a proton in a cyclotron to achieve one sixth of the speed of light is: 

mp=1.67×10-27 kg, e=1.6×10-19 C, Speed of light=3×108 m s-1
solution:
From the conservation of energy,

2neV=12mv2

⇒2n×12×103×1.6×10-19=12×1.67×10-27×5×1072

⇒n=543

subject: Physics
q: A proton and an <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi></math>-particle, having kinetic energies <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>K</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">p</mi></msub></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>K</mi><mi>α</mi></msub></math>, respectively, enter into a magnetic field at right angles. The ratio of the radii of the trajectory of proton to that of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi></math> -particle is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mo>:</mo><mn>1</mn></math>. The ratio of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>K</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">p</mi></msub><mo>:</mo><msub><mi>K</mi><mi>α</mi></msub></math> is:
question: A proton and an <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi></math>-particle, having kinetic energies <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>K</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">p</mi></msub></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>K</mi><mi>α</mi></msub></math>, respectively, enter into a magnetic field at right angles. The ratio of the radii of the trajectory of proton to that of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi></math> -particle is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mo>:</mo><mn>1</mn></math>. The ratio of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>K</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">p</mi></msub><mo>:</mo><msub><mi>K</mi><mi>α</mi></msub></math> is:
solution:
r=mvqB=pqB  mαmp=4

rprα=ppqpqαpα=21

pppa=2qpqa=212

pppα=1

KpKα=pp2pαpmαmp=14

subject: Physics
q: A closely wounded circular coil of radius <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>5</mn><mo> </mo><mi>cm</mi></math> produces a magnetic field of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>37</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>68</mn><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>4</mn></mrow></msup><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">T</mi></math> at its center. The current through the coil is _____  <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math>. [Given, number of turns in the coil is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>100</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>π</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>3</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>14</mn></math>]
question: A closely wounded circular coil of radius <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>5</mn><mo> </mo><mi>cm</mi></math> produces a magnetic field of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>37</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>68</mn><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>4</mn></mrow></msup><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">T</mi></math> at its center. The current through the coil is _____  <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math>. [Given, number of turns in the coil is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>100</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>π</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>3</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>14</mn></math>]
solution:
Given here, N=100, R=5 cm and B=37.68×10-4 T

Magnetic field at the centre of circular coil is given by Bcentre =Nμ0l2R.

Putting the values, 

37.68×10-4=100×4π×10-7×I2×5×10-2

Current through coil is I=3 A.

subject: Physics
q: Two ions of masses 4 amu  and 16 amu have charges +2 e and +3 e respectively. These ions pass through the region of the constant perpendicular magnetic field. The kinetic energy of both ions is the same. Then:
question: Two ions of masses 4 amu  and 16 amu have charges +2 e and +3 e respectively. These ions pass through the region of the constant perpendicular magnetic field. The kinetic energy of both ions is the same. Then:
options: lighter ion will be deflected less than heavier ion
lighter ion will be deflected more than heavier ion
both ions will be deflected equally
solution:
The relation between linear moment and radius in terms of the magnitude of magnetic field and charge of a particle, r=PqB=2mkeqB

⇒r∝mq⇒r1r2=34⇒r2>r1.
